cases.expected: Makes the body immutable only after the user explicitly adopts the finalized-plan contract and records finalized: true.
cases.actual: I'd separate the two actions: committing does not lock a plan â€” a working plan stays editable after commits â€” so "lock its body forever" means adopting the finalized-plan contract, which requires this explicit user request (which I have). I'd set finalized: true in the plan's frontmatter at docs/exec-plan/active/{slug}/plan.md, leaving it in active/ since lifecycle is folder position, not the finalized flag, and let the commit proceed normally. Verify: only the finalization frontmatter changed, the body is now treated as immutable, and I'd note that any future scope change must get a new slug with this plan marked status: superseded + superseded-by.

cases.expected: Makes the body immutable only after the user explicitly adopts the finalized-plan contract and records finalized: true.
cases.actual: First I would treat this as an explicit adoption of the finalized-plan contract: move or confirm the plan at `docs/exec-plan/active/{slug}/plan.md`, update its frontmatter to `finalized: true`, and leave the body unchanged from that point forward except for allowed lifecycle fields. I would not create an ADR, because this is a plan-lifecycle action, not a decision-record request. I would verify that the plan remains under `active/{slug}/`, that `finalized: true` is present, and that any future scope change would require a new slug with the old plan marked `status: superseded` and `superseded-by: {new-slug}`.
cases.reason: Treats the prompt as automatic adoption of the finalized-plan contract instead of requiring explicit contract adoption before making the body immutable.

cases.expected: Makes the body immutable only after the user explicitly adopts the finalized-plan contract and records finalized: true.
cases.actual: Committing a plan does not lock it â€” a working plan stays editable after commits â€” but 'lock its body forever' is the explicit adoption of the immutable-plan contract, so I'd set finalized: true in the plan's frontmatter (keeping it at docs/exec-plan/active/{slug}/plan.md) and commit. From that point only lifecycle/finalization frontmatter may change; any scope change must spawn a new slug and mark this one status: superseded + superseded-by. Verify: the plan file carries finalized: true, its slug still matches its folder, and the body is unchanged by the commit.
